findings.evidence: In DelegatedTaskSandboxOrchestrator::initialize the worktree path is passed as `self.sandbox_path.to_str().unwrap()` inside the `git worktree add` args; a non-UTF-8 PathBuf panics the process.
findings.fix_recipe: Replace the `.args([... self.sandbox_path.to_str().unwrap(), "HEAD"])` call with `Command::new("git").arg("worktree").arg("add").arg(&self.sandbox_path).arg("HEAD")` so the OsStr path is passed directly, or convert via `to_str().ok_or_else(|| io::Error::new(InvalidInput, ...))?`.

findings.evidence: There is no copy-based fallback: when `git worktree add` fails the fallback arm simply calls `std::fs::create_dir_all(&self.sandbox_path)` creating an empty dir. `git log -S "fs::copy"` shows fs::copy has never existed in this file, so the described symlink-following copy walk does not apply.

validate_containment normalizes ParentDir components only for its internal starts_with check and returns `Result<(), AgentError>` (no path); generate_proposal then derives the proposal path from the raw original via `input.target_path.strip_prefix(&self.sandbox_base).unwrap_or(input.target_path)` and wraps it in `CanonicalPath(...)`, so `..` segments persist in the emitted payload.
findings.fix_recipe: Change validate_containment to return the normalized contained PathBuf (the `clean_path`), and in generate_proposal use that normalized path for strip_prefix and the CanonicalPath payload. Reject any resulting relative path whose components include ParentDir/RootDir/Prefix before constructing CreateFileProposal.

findings.evidence: generate_proposal's doc says 'Compares sandbox directory state with HEAD checkout' but the body never reads HEAD/diffs; it unconditionally returns `ProposalPayload::CreateFile` and sets every field of ProposalVersionPreconditions to None (file_version, expected_fingerprint, etc.).
findings.fix_recipe: In generate_proposal, stat/read the base (HEAD or sandbox-base) target: if it exists emit an edit/patch payload, else CreateFile. Populate ProposalVersionPreconditions (expected_fingerprint, expected_file_length, expected_modified_at, file_content_version) from the base/sandbox file metadata to provide a concurrency guard.

findings.evidence: record_proposal_output builds evidence_id `format!("legion-evidence:{}", worker_id.0)` and result_id `format!("legion-result:{}", worker_id.0)` and unconditionally pushes to proposal_outputs/evidence_records/worker_results with no duplicate check, so repeated calls per worker create colliding ids and duplicate results.
findings.fix_recipe: Before pushing, check whether an evidence/result with that id (or the worker_id/proposal_id pair) already exists and return a structured AgentError on duplicate, or make ids unique by including output.proposal_id/output_id in the format string and dedupe proposal_outputs by proposal id.

findings.evidence: provider_route_request_from_worker hardcodes `cancellation_token: CancellationTokenId(uuid::Uuid::from_u128(13))` and `event_sequence: EventSequence(13)` for every worker, so all provider routes share the same cancellation/audit identity.
findings.fix_recipe: Derive cancellation_token and event_sequence from per-worker/session data (e.g. hash worker_id/correlation_id into the Uuid, and use a monotonically increasing session-supplied sequence) rather than the constant 13, or require these as parameters from the caller/worker metadata.

findings.evidence: The legion-agent crate contains only src/lib.rs; dag.rs and the function `workflow_dag_from_approved_plan` exist nowhere in the workspace. `git log -S workflow_dag_from_approved_plan` shows the name appears only in the review doc commit 87c9789, never in source.

No evidence.rs file and no `external_log_evidence_record`/`record_external_log_evidence` functions exist in the workspace; git pickaxe shows these identifiers only in review doc commit 87c9789, never in any .rs source.

No external.rs file and no `validate_workspace_edit_conversion` function exist anywhere; git pickaxe shows the name only in review doc commit 87c9789. The described code was never part of the codebase.
Same nonexistent external.rs / validate_workspace_edit_conversion as Finding 9; the `let _ = principal` validation logic described is not present anywhere in the workspace source.
No plan.rs file and no `task_entries` function exist in the workspace; git pickaxe shows `task_entries` only in review doc commit 87c9789, never in source code.

No scope.rs file and no `target_is_within_scope`/`forbids_path` functions exist in the workspace; git pickaxe shows both names only in review doc commit 87c9789, never in source. (Note: real path-containment logic does exist as validate_containment in lib.rs, but it is unrelated to the cited scope.rs functions.)

After `let completion = provider.complete(...)?;` the success response sets `provider_id: request.provider_id.clone()` and `model_label: request.model_label.clone()` (lines 624-625) with no comparison against `completion.provider`/`completion.model`, which exist on ChatCompletionResponse (lib.rs:104-106).
findings.fix_recipe: In route_completion, after obtaining `completion`, add `if completion.provider != request.provider_id || completion.model != request.model_label { return Ok(self.refused_response(&request, "provider.identity_mismatch", "provider returned a different provider/model than requested")); }` (or return a ProviderError). Optionally also record the actual provider/model from `completion` in the response instead of echoing the request.

findings.evidence: `allowed_route_decision()` hard-codes `schema_version: 1` (line 691) while the enclosing route response sets `schema_version: request.schema_version` (line 632); the nested AssistedAiRouteDecision can therefore be stale.
findings.fix_recipe: Change signature to `fn allowed_route_decision(schema_version: u16) -> AssistedAiRouteDecision` and use that parameter for the nested `schema_version`; at the call site (line 623) pass `request.schema_version`.

findings.evidence: deterministic_inline_prediction validates request metadata first (lib.rs:404) and validate_inline_prediction_request_metadata rejects `max_prediction_bytes == 0` (protocol:10774); the built result is then validated (lib.rs:457) and validate_inline_prediction_ghost_text rejects empty text (protocol:11613), so an Available result with empty ghost text can never be returned (the function returns Err instead).

redaction_patterns now use regexes matching the entire secret (e.g. `authorization:\s*bearer\s+\S+`, `(?:openai_api_key|...|api_key)\s*=\s*\S+`, `(?:sk-|...)\S+`) and replace_all collapses marker+value; tests assert the full value (`sk-test-mock-value`, `mock-bearer-value`) is gone (lines 99-102).
Every redaction regex spec is prefixed with the case-insensitive flag `(?i)` (lines 22,24,26,28), so mixed/upper/lowercase markers are all replaced rather than merely flagged.

flush_code no longer guards on empty code_lines; it always pushes a `MarkdownStreamSegment::CodeBlock` preserving language and complete flag (lines 52-56), with a comment documenting empty blocks are intentionally preserved, and dedicated tests cover empty complete and incomplete fences (lines 117-148).

The cited file crates/legion-ai/src/telemetry.rs does not exist and has no git history; legion-ai contains only lib.rs, redaction.rs, streaming.rs and no telemetry code (grep for `telemetry` in lib.rs returns nothing). The described Option<HostedTelemetrySpoolRecord> helper collapsing validation/consent into None could not be located in current code.
findings.fix_recipe: Locate the actual telemetry helper (possibly relocated to legion-telemetry/legion-observability) before acting; if it exists, return Result<Option<...>, _> so validation/construction errors are distinguishable from consent-policy None.

findings.evidence: All four blocking transports call `reqwest::blocking::Client::new().post(...)` with no `.timeout()`/`.connect_timeout()` (ReqwestProviderHttpTransport:303, Anthropic post_json:1102, post_text:1139, StreamableHttpMcpTransport:2059), so a hung endpoint blocks the calling thread indefinitely.
findings.fix_recipe: Construct a shared `reqwest::blocking::Client` via `Client::builder().connect_timeout(Duration::from_secs(...)).timeout(Duration::from_secs(...)).build()` (stored once per transport or in a OnceLock) and reuse it at all four sites; map elapsed timeouts to ProviderError::RequestFailed/McpClientError::Transport; add a test with a delayed/injected transport.

findings.evidence: The chat/completions payload serializes `"max_tokens": request.max_tokens` and `"temperature": request.temperature` directly inside `json!` (lines 853-854), so when the options are None they are sent as JSON null rather than omitted.
findings.fix_recipe: Build the payload as a mutable `json!({...})` without max_tokens/temperature, then `if let Some(v) = request.max_tokens { payload["max_tokens"] = json!(v); }` and likewise for temperature, mirroring the incremental pattern already used in OpenAiResponsesProvider::request_payload (lines 571-576).

findings.evidence: Both Anthropic transport methods send the credential via `request.bearer_auth(token)` (lines 1110, 1147), and from_env collapses ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN into a single `api_key` field (1179-1186); API keys are therefore sent as a bearer header instead of the required `x-api-key`.
findings.fix_recipe: Track credential kind (ApiKey vs AuthToken) when reading env in from_env, plumb it through with_transport/AnthropicMessagesClient, and in post_json/post_text set `request.header("x-api-key", key)` for API keys and `request.bearer_auth(token)` only for auth tokens; add tests asserting the header chosen per credential type.

findings.evidence: `"store": Self::metadata_flag(&request.metadata, "openai.responses.store", true)` defaults the Responses-API `store` flag to true (line 569; metadata_flag returns the default when the key is absent), opting into provider-side response storage unless callers override it.
findings.fix_recipe: Change the default argument at line 569 from `true` to `false` so provider-side storage requires an explicit `openai.responses.store=true` metadata opt-in; update/add a test asserting `store` defaults to false and document retention behavior.

findings.evidence: send_on_session loops on `read_line` until `response.get("id") == expected_id` with no timeout, deadline, or max-iteration bound (lines 1977-1998); the session mutex is locked in `send` (line 2008) and held across this unbounded blocking read, so a wedged or notification-only server hangs the caller forever.
findings.fix_recipe: Add a per-request deadline (e.g. Instant::now()+timeout) to the loop and return McpClientError::Transport on expiry; set a read timeout on the underlying stdout/child pipe or bound the number of unrelated messages; handle JSON-RPC error responses/notifications explicitly; consider not holding the session mutex across the blocking read.

findings.evidence: main() defaults `path` to "scratch.txt" then calls `app.open_file(path)?`; `open_file` forwards `OpenFileIntent::Existing` (lib.rs:13206), and the Existing arm calls `open_existing_file_text_with_causality` (lib.rs:8183) which reads metadata, so a missing scratch.txt aborts startup with `?`.
findings.fix_recipe: In main(), when the path argument is absent (the scratch.txt default) and the file does not exist, call `app.open_new_file(path)` (CreateNew intent) instead of `open_file`; or detect NotFound from open_file and retry via open_new_file, or require the user to pass an existing path.

findings.evidence: The `#[cfg(test)]` helper `route_request()` builds `AssistedAiProviderRouteRequest { route_id: ..., ... schema_version: 1 }` with no `prompt_prefix`, while the field is required (legion-protocol/src/lib.rs:6657 `pub prompt_prefix: String`) and the non-test builder sets it (offline_ai.rs:1105). offline_ai is `#[cfg(not(feature="ai"))]` (lib.rs:26) and default=["ai"], so `--no-default-features --all-targets` compiles this test and fails with E0063.
findings.fix_recipe: Add `prompt_prefix: String::new(),` (or a deterministic prefix) to the struct literal in `route_request()` at offline_ai.rs:1283, and keep `--no-default-features --all-targets` in CI.

findings.evidence: validate_containment canonicalizes base via `std::fs::canonicalize(base)` but only lexically normalizes the candidate (the `clean_components` loop strips ParentDir/CurDir without resolving symlinks) before `clean_stripped.starts_with(&base_stripped)`; it also calls `std::env::current_dir().unwrap()` at lines 412 and 417, panicking when the cwd is unavailable instead of returning OfflineAiError.
findings.fix_recipe: Replace both `std::env::current_dir().unwrap()` calls with `std::env::current_dir().map_err(|e| OfflineAiError::InvalidMetadata(...))?` (change the fn to propagate). Resolve the candidate's existing parent directory with `canonicalize` and join the final component, or reject any symlink components, so a symlink inside the sandbox cannot escape; then compare canonical paths.

findings.evidence: `crates/legion-app/src/proposal.rs` does not exist (src/ contains only lib.rs, main.rs, offline_ai.rs) and has no git history; `filtered_batch_proposal_for_accepted_targets` / `filtered_batch` / `for_accepted` match nowhere in crates/. The current batch code (apply_batch_proposal, preflight at lib.rs:22921-22939, batch_has_dependency_cycle at 23341) validates dependency edges but contains no accepted-target filtering that drops edges, so the described defect cannot be located in the current tree.

After `workspace.save_file_with_proposal` returns Ok(applied) (lib.rs:10270), an `observe_proposal_response` audit failure returns `SaveWorkflowFailure` (lib.rs:10279-10282) with no rollback of the on-disk write; save_buffer's Err arm reports `AppSaveOutcome::Rejected` and never calls `bind_saved_buffer` (only the Ok arm does, line 17678), so disk is mutated while the buffer stays dirty. The generic apply path instead calls `rollback_audit_failed_mutation` on audit failure (lib.rs:21275).
findings.fix_recipe: In SaveWorkflowService::save_active_buffer Ok-branch, when observe_proposal_response returns Err, roll back the just-written file using a pre-save checkpoint (mirror `rollback_audit_failed_mutation`/`rollback_snapshot_for_proposal`) before returning the failure; alternatively persist the audit before committing the workspace save, or commit saved metadata while surfacing the audit failure separately so disk and editor state stay consistent.

findings.evidence: After `orchestrator.initialize(&permission)?` (lib.rs:15280) allocates the sandbox, the ACP path's `acp_host_command.run(...).map_err(...)?` (15325-15329) and `std::fs::read_to_string(&target_file_absolute)?` (15365-15367) return early without `orchestrator.cleanup`, whereas the unsuccessful-exit branch (15356) and the post-generation path (15391) do clean up, confirming the inconsistency.
findings.fix_recipe: Wrap the post-initialize ACP block so every early return runs `orchestrator.cleanup(&permission)` (a scope-guard/RAII drop wrapper around the orchestrator, or explicit cleanup before the `run(...)?` and `read_to_string(...)?` error returns), and surface cleanup errors alongside the original error.

findings.evidence: No filtered/partial delegated-apply code exists: `filtered_batch`/`build_filtered`/`filter_proposal` match nowhere in crates/, and `filtered_apply_required` appears only as a projection flag computed by `from_hunks` (lib.rs:828). `review_delegate_proposal_hunk` (lib.rs:18759-18794) merely records dispositions into `hunk_decisions` and returns a projection; lib.rs:21700-21763 is workspace-reopen/save-commit code, not a filtered apply. proposal_ledger_projection does render from `self.proposals` (lib.rs:2163), but with no subset-apply path the described divergence cannot be located.

findings.title: No workspace rust-version (MSRV) declared for edition 2024
findings.evidence: [workspace.package] declares version, edition="2024", license, publish=false but no rust-version; grep for 'rust-version' across Cargo.toml returns no matches.
findings.fix_recipe: Add `rust-version = "1.85"` (or the chosen MSRV that supports edition 2024 and current deps) to [workspace.package] in Cargo.toml, have member crates inherit via `rust-version.workspace = true`, and add a CI lane pinning that toolchain to enforce the floor.

findings.title: Lockfile contains many duplicate crate versions
findings.evidence: `grep '^name = ' Cargo.lock | sort | uniq -d | wc -l` reports 44 duplicated crate names; windows-sys resolves to 0.45.0/0.52.0/0.59.0/0.60.2/0.61.2 and bitflags/thiserror each appear twice (some specifics like toml now resolve to a single 0.8.23, so the review's exact list has drifted).
findings.fix_recipe: Run `cargo tree -d` to enumerate duplicates, align direct dependency versions in [workspace.dependencies] where possible, document unavoidable transitive duplicates with `[bans] skip`/`skip-tree` entries in deny.toml, then promote multiple-versions to deny once the baseline is minimized.

findings.title: Yanked/unknown-registry/unknown-git set to warn instead of deny
findings.evidence: deny.toml has `yanked = "warn"` (line 10), `unknown-registry = "warn"` (line 52), and `unknown-git = "warn"` (line 53), so the cargo-deny gate passes even with yanked or off-policy sources.
findings.fix_recipe: Change `yanked`, `unknown-registry`, and `unknown-git` to "deny" in deny.toml; add `allow-registry`/`allow-git` allowlists for the known-good sources, and use narrow documented ignore entries (with owner/removal note) for any temporary exceptions. Note: CI workflow currently does not run cargo-deny since the workflow was removed (see CI findings), so enforcement also depends on restoring a gate.

findings.title: multiple-versions = warn with no skip/exception list
findings.evidence: [bans] block has `multiple-versions = "warn"` and `wildcards = "allow"` with no `skip`/`skip-tree` entries, while the lockfile holds ~44 duplicate crate names.
findings.fix_recipe: Add `[[bans.skip]]`/`[[bans.skip-tree]]` entries with rationale comments for each unavoidable duplicate in deny.toml, resolve avoidable duplicates by aligning versions, then set `multiple-versions = "deny"` once the baseline is clean.

findings.title: Floating stable toolchain not pinned to rust-toolchain.toml/MSRV
findings.evidence: The workflow installing the floating toolchain was deleted (commit d397f5e), and no rust-toolchain.toml exists; the time-dependent CI behavior described no longer applies to any present file. (Related MSRV gap is captured separately under Cargo.toml Finding 1.)
`TimestampMillis::now()` does `SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).map_or(0, |d| d.as_millis() as u64)` — pre-epoch clocks collapse to 0 and the u128->u64 cast can wrap silently.
findings.fix_recipe: In TimestampMillis::now(), replace `d.as_millis() as u64` with `u64::try_from(d.as_millis()).unwrap_or(u64::MAX)`; optionally add a fallible `try_now() -> Result<Self,_>` that surfaces the `duration_since` error instead of `map_or(0, ...)`.

findings.evidence: Protected-path gate uses substring match: `.any(|protected| !protected.is_empty() && target.contains(protected))` over `protected_path_patterns`, which is not path-aware (separators/case/canonicalization/globs ignored).
findings.fix_recipe: In evaluate_delegated_task_runtime_readiness, normalize each target label to a canonical Path and compare protected patterns with component/boundary-aware matching (or compiled globs), e.g. `Path::starts_with` on canonicalized components, keeping a conservative fallback for non-path labels rather than raw `contains`.

findings.evidence: lib.rs:18 declares `pub mod capability;` and lib.rs:27 re-exports `AssistedAiCapabilityMatrix`, but no capability.rs/capability/mod.rs exists in the repo or git history; `cargo check -p legion-protocol` fails with E0583 'file not found for module `capability`', and `has_explicit_declaration` is not defined anywhere in lib.rs.

findings.evidence: lib.rs:20 declares `pub mod plan;` and lib.rs:28-32 re-exports `EditablePlanArtifact`, but no plan.rs exists in repo or git history; `cargo check` fails E0583 for `plan`, and `EditablePlanArtifact::validate` is not defined in lib.rs.

findings.evidence: lib.rs:24 declares `pub mod scope;` and lib.rs:33 re-exports `DelegatedTaskScope`, but no scope.rs exists in repo or git history; `cargo check` fails E0583 for `scope`, and `target_is_within_scope`/`forbidden_paths` are not defined in lib.rs.
findings.fix_recipe: Cannot validate: locate/restore the scope module, then add an `allows_target_path()` predicate combining workspace boundary, target kind, and `forbidden_paths`, or have `target_is_within_scope()` call `forbids_path()` and return false on a forbidden match.

findings.evidence: lib.rs:26 declares `pub mod tools;` and lib.rs:34-38 re-exports `LegionToolCallFeedback`/`LegionToolKind`, but no tools.rs exists (untracked, absent); `cargo check` fails E0583 for `tools`, and these types have no definition in lib.rs.

findings.evidence: The read/edit-as-proposal JSON schemas live in the missing tools.rs (lib.rs:26 `pub mod tools;` references a nonexistent file; `cargo check` fails E0583); the schema text constraining start_line/end_line cannot be located in current source.
findings.fix_recipe: Cannot validate: locate/restore the tools module, then add post-schema runtime validation rejecting `start_line > end_line` and returning structured `InvalidArguments` feedback for inverted ranges.

findings.evidence: high_dpi_status matches `Some(scale) if scale > 1.0 => format!("os-observed scale {scale:.3}")` and falls through `Some(_) | None => NOT_OBSERVED`, so an observed scale of exactly 1.0 is reported as 'not observed'.
findings.fix_recipe: In high_dpi_status, split the match: `Some(scale) if scale.is_finite() && scale > 0.0 => format!("os-observed scale {scale:.3}")` for any positive finite scale (including 1.0), and reserve NOT_OBSERVED for `None` and non-finite/<=0 values.

findings.evidence: temporary_session_path builds `.{file_name}.{process::id()}.{suffix}` using only the PID, and save_crash_safe does `let _ = fs::remove_file(&temp_path)` before writing, so two concurrent saves of the same path in one process collide and can delete each other's temp.
findings.fix_recipe: Add a per-save uniqueness component to temporary_session_path: append an atomic counter (static AtomicU64::fetch_add) and/or a random nonce in addition to process::id(), or switch to a tempfile crate NamedTempFile in the destination dir. Remove the blind remove_file or scope it to the unique nonce only.

findings.evidence: reject_raw_source_markers does `json.contains(**marker)` over the whole serialized document for markers like `source_body`/`small_buffer_preview`, so any metadata field, path, or label containing those substrings fails the save/load.
findings.fix_recipe: Deserialize to the record and inspect only the known raw-payload-carrying fields (e.g. preview/body fields) rather than substring-scanning the entire JSON, or use a dedicated schema-level redaction marker that cannot collide with user content.

findings.evidence: `let base_branch = snapshot.git_projection.remote_default_branch.as_deref().unwrap_or(branch_label);` falls back to the current branch when the default branch is missing, producing a branch...branch self-compare URL.
findings.fix_recipe: Replace the unwrap_or(branch_label) fallback: if remote_default_branch is None/empty, return a typed error (e.g. add DesktopBridgeError::MissingRemoteDefaultBranch) instead of defaulting to the current branch; only use a configured validated fallback when the projection explicitly provides one.

findings.evidence: On Ok the handler only calls `self.set_status(...Opened...)` and returns WorkspaceOpened; it never assigns `self.workspace_root = root`, so diagnostics/health/session paths keep the original root (field defined at line 530/605).
findings.fix_recipe: In the OpenWorkspace Ok branch, set `self.workspace_root = root.clone();` and reset workspace-scoped adapter-local explorer/expansion state; ensure session_state/diagnostics paths derived from workspace_root are rebased or explicitly retained per launch policy.

findings.evidence: Multiple UI paths discard the Result: `let _ = self.runtime.handle_action(action)` (2201, 2348), `let _ = self.runtime.handle_action(DesktopAction::UpdatePaletteQuery...)` (2259-2261), and palette selection actions (2323-2329).
findings.fix_recipe: Capture each handle_action Result; on Err set an error status (self.set_status(StatusSeverity::Error, ...)) and request a projection refresh/repaint. For headless/test harness paths, store or propagate the error so failures are assertable.

findings.evidence: editor_text_input_actions computes `at` once before iterating events, and every Text/Paste/ImeCommit action pushed in the loop reuses the same stale `at`, so multiple text events in one frame all target the original cursor offset.
findings.fix_recipe: Either concatenate consecutive Text/Paste payloads into one InsertText at `at` preserving order, or track a running insertion offset locally (advance `at` by the inserted length after each generated action) so batched actions target sequential positions; ideally refresh the projection cursor between dispatches.

findings.evidence: On Windows it builds `cmd /C start ""` then `command.arg(url)`, passing the URL as a raw cmd argument; `&` and other metacharacters in forge/GitLab MR URLs are reinterpreted by cmd.
findings.fix_recipe: Avoid cmd /C start for URLs: use the `open` crate (open::that) or call ShellExecuteW directly via windows-sys, or at minimum escape cmd metacharacters (^& etc.) in the URL before passing to cmd. Prefer a ShellExecute-based opener that does not go through the cmd parser.

findings.evidence: `format!("workspace: {}", self.workspace_label)` and `format!("unsupported: {surface}")` (and markdown equivalents `workspace_label:`/`- {surface}`) interpolate labels with no newline/control-char normalization or markdown escaping.
findings.fix_recipe: Add a normalization helper that replaces \r\n\t and other control chars (and escapes markdown-sensitive chars for to_markdown) and apply it to workspace_label and each unsupported surface before formatting rows/markdown.

findings.evidence: to_markdown emits only metadata/count fields and ends with a Privacy section stating diagnostics exclude raw bodies; there is no triple-backtick fence or opt-in raw snapshot appendix anywhere in the file (grep for ``` and {:#?}/raw appendix found none). FrameTimingRecorder stores input_paint_samples and frame_durations_ms as plain Vecs with unconditional `.push(...)` and no eviction, so a long-running session grows them unbounded and summary() sorts/iterates ever-larger vectors.

findings.evidence: The result row `format!("{}:{}:{}{} {}", path, line, char, truncated, row.snippet)` interpolates row.snippet directly, so newlines/tabs/control chars in the snippet break the one-line row format.

findings.evidence: cargo_args returns `["build","-p","legion-desktop"]` with no --target triple, and executable_source = workspace_root/target/<profile>/WINDOWS_EXECUTABLE_NAME; on a non-Windows host this builds the host binary and points at a path that has no .exe.

findings.evidence: prepare_beta_workspace resolves workspace_root from `std::env::current_dir()` (346-349) and joins relative beta paths against it (`workspace_root.join(path)` 359), ignoring BetaWorkflowConfig.real_workspace_root.

findings.evidence: beta_smoke_command builds `format!("--workspace {}", config.real_workspace_root.display())` etc. and `.join(" ")` with no quoting, so paths with spaces/metacharacters produce a non-rerunnable command.

findings.evidence: Only `if observations.frame_count == 0 { errors.push(...) }` gates status; adapter checks, focus/high-DPI, accessibility projection, and guardrail statuses can be 'not observed'/failed while status stays RendererSmokeStatus::Passed.

findings.evidence: `match self.pixels_per_point { Some(scale) if scale > 1.0 => ..., Some(_) | None => NOT_OBSERVED }` reports an observed scale of exactly 1.0 as 'not observed', same false-negative as platform.rs.

findings.evidence: drag_selection_range builds `ProtocolTextRange { start: drag_anchor.unwrap_or(current_cursor), end: coordinate }` with no ordering normalization; the emitted range flows through set_buffer_selection -> editor_range -> set_selections (legion-editor lib.rs:1880-1891) which stores `state.selections = selections` verbatim, so a backwards drag yields an inverted range. (The review's named paint fn `selection_span_for_line` does not exist, but the inverted-range defect is real.)
findings.fix_recipe: In drag_selection_range, after picking start=anchor/cursor and end=coordinate, compare the two TextCoordinates (by line then character) and swap so start<=end before constructing ProtocolTextRange; alternatively add a `normalized()` helper on ProtocolTextRange and call it at all SetSelection emit sites (1909-1918, 1930-1933) and in set_buffer_selection.

findings.evidence: read_until_correlated_response has `if id != target_json_rpc_id { // Skip notification-shaped or out-of-order responses. continue; }`, so a response carrying another pending json_rpc_id is dropped without being correlated or buffered, leaving that request stranded in pending_by_json_rpc_id.
findings.fix_recipe: In read_until_correlated_response, when `id` is present, always call self.client.correlate_response(envelope) and store/queue completed LspCorrelatedResponse by request id in a new map on LspStdioSession; return immediately if it matches expected_request_id, otherwise stash it. Have read_response_for first check that stash before reading new frames. Alternatively serialize one in-flight request at a time.

findings.evidence: read_response_for just calls read_until_correlated_response, which loops on self.process.read_envelope()? with no deadline; LspPendingRequest.timeout_ms and LspClient::resolve_timeout (lib.rs:882) are never consulted on this path.
findings.fix_recipe: Add a deadline derived from pending.timeout_ms around the read loop (e.g. a nonblocking/timed read or watchdog thread on the LspStdioProcess). On expiry call self.client.resolve_timeout(pending.request_id, elapsed_ms), optionally emit $/cancelRequest, and return the Timeout-status LspCorrelatedResponse instead of blocking until EOF.

findings.evidence: prepare_request does `self.next_json_rpc_id = self.next_json_rpc_id.saturating_add(1);`, so once it reaches u64::MAX every later request reuses MAX and overwrites pending_by_json_rpc_id / json_rpc_id_by_request_id entries.
findings.fix_recipe: Change prepare_request to use checked_add(1) and return an explicit exhaustion LspRuntimeError (or change prepare_request signature to LspRuntimeResult), so id reuse cannot silently corrupt correlation tables.

findings.evidence: completion_projection_for_item computes `score_basis_points: 10_000u16.saturating_sub((index as u16).saturating_mul(100))`; for index >= 65536 the `index as u16` cast wraps (e.g. 65536 -> 0 -> score 10000) before the multiply, so far-down completions regain top scores.
findings.fix_recipe: Compute the penalty in usize/u32: `let penalty = (index as u32).saturating_mul(100); score = 10_000u32.saturating_sub(penalty)` then cast the clamped result to u16.

findings.evidence: read_frame parses Content-Length and then does `let mut payload = vec![0u8; length];` with no maximum bound, unlike the production read_lsp_frame which checks LspFramer::MAX_FRAME_PAYLOAD_BYTES.
findings.fix_recipe: After parsing `length`, reject `length > LspFramer::MAX_FRAME_PAYLOAD_BYTES` (or a small mock-specific constant) returning MockIoError::Protocol before allocating the payload buffer.

findings.evidence: `id` is Option<u64>; the `other =>` arm always emits `"error": ...` with `"id": id`, which serializes to `"id": null` for notifications (no id), violating the JSON-RPC rule that notifications get no response.
findings.fix_recipe: In the unknown-method (`other`) arm, if `id` is None return None (no response). More generally, gate response emission on `id.is_some()` for request-only methods and only handle true notifications when id is absent.

findings.evidence: handle_transport_envelope dedups/audits on envelope.operation_id and passes it to handlers, but handle_filesystem_operation only checks `operation.operation_id.0 == 0` and handle_process_descriptor only checks `descriptor.operation_id.0 == 0`; validate_envelope (918) never compares the payload's embedded operation_id to envelope.operation_id.
findings.fix_recipe: In validate_envelope or each handler, require `operation.operation_id == envelope.operation_id` (and apply the same session/principal/correlation/causality consistency checks) before dispatching; return RemoteRuntimeError::InvalidOperation on mismatch.

findings.evidence: validate_mutation_gate only checks proposal_id present, preconditions.has_required_write_guards() (which merely requires granted+nonzero decision_id and a non-empty principal, protocol lib.rs:2330-2339) and workspace_generation; it never calls validate_capability for "remote.fs.write" nor compares preconditions.principal_id to the session/envelope principal.
findings.fix_recipe: In validate_mutation_gate, call validate_capability(&preconditions.capability_decision, "remote.fs.write") and return denied on error; compare preconditions.principal_id against the active session/envelope principal; and bind preconditions.correlation_id/causality_id to the operation envelope, denying on mismatch.

findings.evidence: create_file builds `content = format!("remote-created-by-proposal:{}", proposal_id.0)` but stores `fingerprint: metadata_fingerprint("remote-created-by-proposal")`, fingerprinting the constant string rather than the actual content.

findings.evidence: write_file sets `entry.snapshot_id = preconditions.snapshot_id`, create_file sets `snapshot_id: preconditions.snapshot_id`, and rename_file removes/reinserts the old entry unchanged, so no new post-mutation snapshot id is allocated.

findings.evidence: common_headers wraps both AUTHORIZATION and X-Legion-Client-Identity in `if let Ok(header_value) = HeaderValue::from_str(...)`, silently dropping the header when the configured token/identity is invalid, producing an unauthenticated/misattributed request.

findings.evidence: validate_cloud_task_id only rejects empty/whitespace ids; the HTTP transport methods format `task_id.0` straight into paths like `/v1/cloud/tasks/{}/events`, so ids containing `/`, `?`, `#` alter the endpoint path.

findings.evidence: On accept, seen_operations.insert and inflight_operations.insert grow per operation while only accepted_sequences is trimmed to replay_window_size (lines 734-736); seen_operations is never evicted, and replay_window reports accepted_operation_count = seen_operations.len() (line 909).
findings.fix_recipe: Track operation ids together with their sequence and evict seen_operations entries whose sequence falls below the lowest retained accepted_sequence when the replay window slides; keep inflight_operations separate (already removed on ack) and base replay-window counts on the bounded set.

findings.evidence: checkpoint only validates session match, event_sequence != 0, and that last_operation_id is in seen_operations; it never checks checkpoint.event_sequence against self.last_sequence or the accepted replay window before storing last_checkpoint.
findings.fix_recipe: In checkpoint, reject when `checkpoint.event_sequence.0 > self.last_sequence.0` or when it is below the lowest retained accepted_sequence (outside the replay window) with RemoteTransportCoreError::ReplayRejected before assigning self.last_checkpoint.

findings.evidence: connect_inner calls validate_remote_transport_connection_attempt before the addition, and that validator rejects `attempt.timeout_ms == 0 || attempt.timeout_ms > 120_000`, so `Instant::now() + Duration::from_millis(timeout_ms)` is bounded to <=120s and cannot overflow.

metadata_hash builds the digest with `let mut hasher = DefaultHasher::new(); value.hash(&mut hasher); format!("{:016x}", hasher.finish())` and metadata_fingerprint stores that 64-bit non-cryptographic value into FileFingerprint while labeling algorithm with domain strings like "assisted-ai-provider"/"assisted-ai-request" (lines 672-700). findings.evidence: close()/kill() call platform_session_id() (locks, clones id, drops lock), then run the backend op, then remove_session() reacquires the lock; with &self methods a concurrent input() can fetch the same platform id between the backend op and removal. No Closing/Killing state guards the window.
findings.fix_recipe: Hold the registry lock across the lifecycle transition or add a per-session state (Closing/Killing) set under the lock before the backend call so concurrent input/resize/poll return a stable structured error; remove/mark the session atomically before irreversible terminate.

findings.evidence: No `TerminalSessionMetadata::apply_shell_projection` exists. On encountering ESC ] the loop scans for BEL/ST; if none is found cursor advances to bytes.len() and the remainder is taken as `sequence` (line 5256) and never appended to visible_output, so an unterminated/split OSC drops all trailing bytes; the parser is stateless across calls.
findings.fix_recipe: When no terminator is found, either emit the unterminated bytes (from ESC]) back into visible_output, or make the parser hold a per-session carry-over buffer that prepends leftover bytes to the next chunk so split OSC sequences complete. findings.evidence: docs_hygiene_checks_untracked_markdown_in_git_repo runs `Command::new("git").arg("init")...output().expect("git init should run")` (302-306) and the git add (312-318) but never checks output.status.success(); if git fails the test proceeds against a non-git directory and no longer validates untracked-file detection.
findings.fix_recipe: Capture each Command output and assert output.status.success(), printing stdout/stderr on failure (as other git fixture helpers do) before asserting the untracked NEW.md violation.

findings.evidence: perf_harness_unreachable_budget_marks_measurement_failed sets budget_millis=0 and only asserts `assert_ne!(measurement.status, SkeletonStatus::Failed)` (line 162); perf_harness_tight_budget_classifies_measurement_failed also sets budget 0, asserts status==Skipped (179) and merely references `let _ = SkeletonStatus::Failed;` (181). Neither asserts a Failed measurement, so the failure-classification path is untested despite the names.
findings.fix_recipe: Add a deterministic helper/fixture that produces a measurement whose elapsed exceeds its budget (e.g. inject a recorded elapsed time) and assert measurement.status == SkeletonStatus::Failed plus the expected failure message, instead of asserting Skipped.

findings.evidence: perf_harness_fail_on_budget_env_overrides_descriptor_budget mutates process-global env via `unsafe { std::env::set_var(FAIL_ON_BUDGET_ENV, "0") }` (192-194) and restore set_var/remove_var (202-207) with only a comment claiming single-thread safety; cargo runs integration tests concurrently, so another test reading FAIL_ON_BUDGET_ENV can race this mutation.
findings.fix_recipe: Serialize env-mutating tests with a global Mutex or the serial_test attribute, or refactor apply_fail_on_budget_override to accept an injected env value so the test sets no process-global variable.

findings.evidence: TempRepo::new builds its root from `name` plus `SystemTime::now()...as_nanos()` only (lines 20-24); there is no process id or atomic counter, so parallel/rapid same-name construction can collide when clock granularity is coarser than nanoseconds.
findings.fix_recipe: Include std::process::id() and a static AtomicU64 counter (as docs_hygiene/path_boundary fixtures do) in the temp dir name to guarantee uniqueness.

findings.evidence: The cited file crates/legion-ui/src/projection.rs does not exist (legion-ui has only lib.rs and ui.rs), and the function legion_workflow_fleet_card_projections appears nowhere in the source tree nor in git history. The actual proposal-card rendering (render_proposal_cards in crates/legion-desktop/src/view.rs:3259) does not attach any per-proposal verification/test_status label at all.
findings.fix_recipe: Cannot validate: cited file and function do not exist in the current tree or git history. If a per-proposal verification status feature is later added, ensure verification rows are joined to their owning proposal id rather than projecting a single global aggregate onto every card.

findings.evidence: Shell::render starts with print!("\x1b[2J\x1b[H") then emits untrusted content verbatim: println!("{}", text) for buffer text, println!("{}", slice.visible_text) per viewport line, println!("Path: {}", path), tab titles, prompts, debug frames, etc., with zero control-character escaping.
findings.fix_recipe: Add a sanitization helper (strip/escape C0/C1 control chars and ESC sequences, e.g. replace bytes < 0x20 except newline/tab and 0x7f-0x9f) and route every untrusted projection string (visible_text, file_path, tab titles, prompt.message, debug labels, terminal rows) through it before println!. Add tests feeding ANSI/control payloads in buffer text and paths.

findings.evidence: In parse_pos the viewport branch accumulates current_offset from slice.visible_text.len()+1 starting at 0, compares against byte_offset, and returns protocol_text_coordinate(line, character, Some(byte_offset as u64)); it never consults slice.byte_range.start, so the returned byte offset is viewport-relative rather than absolute.
findings.fix_recipe: In the viewport loop, when the target slice is found compute the absolute offset as slice.byte_range.start + (byte_offset - current_offset), validate it lies within [slice.byte_range.start, slice.byte_range.end], and return that as byte_offset; reject offsets that fall outside any visible slice instead of falling through to (0,0).

findings.evidence: parse_pos small-buffer branch uses text.as_bytes().get(..byte_offset) and on None does unwrap_or_else(|| protocol_text_coordinate(0,0,Some(0))); a byte_offset past end silently coerces to file start. (Note: byte-slice get does not reject mid-codepoint offsets, so the char-boundary case is silently mis-counted rather than fallback.)
findings.fix_recipe: Before slicing, validate byte_offset <= text.len() and text.is_char_boundary(byte_offset); if either fails, return a command/validation error (or Noop) rather than coercing to (0,0,0). Add the same char-boundary guard to the viewport branch.

findings.evidence: parse_proposal_id parses u64 and maps to ProposalId with no zero filter, unlike parse_buffer_id (line 4760) which uses .filter(|value| *value != 0). ProposalId(0) is used as a sentinel in empty_approval_checklist_projection (4945) and empty_checkpoint_rollback_projection (4980), so :proposal-approve 0 emits a sentinel-id intent.
findings.fix_recipe: Add .filter(|value| *value != 0) to parse_proposal_id between parse and map(ProposalId), mirroring parse_buffer_id. Add tests asserting zero/empty proposal ids yield None/Noop.

findings.evidence: toast_id(severity, message) is a pure FNV hash over only the severity byte and message bytes, and ToastProjection::from_status_message sets id: toast_id(message.severity, &message.message); two identical status messages produce identical ids, so dismissing one dismisses all and duplicates collapse.
findings.fix_recipe: Incorporate a per-message discriminator (status message index/sequence, timestamp, or source id) into ToastProjection::id while keeping deterministic ids only where dedup is explicitly intended; thread that value through from_status_messages_with_verbosity into from_status_message.

findings.evidence: prepare_batch_edit_plan sorts ascending then reverses (applied descending); in apply_edits each delta records ByteRange::new(prepared.start, changed_end) using the original/high offset before lower-offset edits are applied, then deltas.reverse(); lower edits that change length shift the higher delta's final position but the recorded range is never adjusted.
findings.fix_recipe: Track a running cumulative length delta from already-applied (lower-processed) edits, or recompute each ChangedDelta's byte_range/utf16_range against the final staged_buffer after the loop completes; alternatively accumulate edits ascending and offset previously-recorded ranges by each edit's (new_text.len() - (end-start)).

findings.evidence: set_cursors and set_selections only look up the buffer state then assign state.cursors = cursors / state.selections = selections with no position validation; viewport_projection later calls protocol_range(&state.buffer, selection.range) and state.buffer.try_byte_offset(cursor)? (lines 1044-1046) which return Err for invalid positions, making projection fallible for stored state.
findings.fix_recipe: In set_cursors/set_selections, validate every Cursor.position and Selection.range via buffer.try_byte_offset (and start<=end) before storing, returning EditorError on invalid input so only valid editor state is ever persisted.

findings.evidence: EditorRequest::ApplyTransaction fetches buffer_metadata, checks workspace_id/file_id match, then returns Ok(EditorResponse::Transaction(descriptor)) with no buffer mutation, no transaction-log append, and no event emission; callers get a success for an unapplied transaction.
findings.fix_recipe: Either route this through real mutation (resolve the descriptor's edits/preconditions and call apply_edits with transaction/event recording) or return ProtocolError::unsupported when no mutation occurs; do not return EditorResponse::Transaction for a pure identity check.

findings.evidence: EditorRequest::Overlay(overlay) => Ok(EditorResponse::OverlayApplied(overlay.overlay_id)) returns success without ever calling engine.set_overlays (defined at 1894) or otherwise updating any buffer's state.overlays.
findings.fix_recipe: Add/resolve a buffer id on the overlay request and call engine.set_overlays(buffer_id, ...) (or merge into the target buffer's overlay list) before returning OverlayApplied; if overlays are projection-only, return ProtocolError::unsupported instead.

findings.evidence: request_save matches try_full_text() and on Err(FullCacheBudgetExceeded) calls snapshot.materialize_full_text_from_chunks()? to build a whole-file String on the interactive path; EditorError::DegradedSaveUnavailable is defined but grep shows it is never returned anywhere, contradicting the viewport message that 'Phase 1 saves fail closed'.
findings.fix_recipe: Pick one contract: to fail closed, return EditorError::DegradedSaveUnavailable(buffer_id) in the FullCacheBudgetExceeded arm instead of materializing; to support degraded saves, stream chunks through the save payload without a single String and update the large-file status/error text accordingly.

findings.evidence: LineIndex::visible_line_slices only checks start_line > end_line_exclusive, then does Vec::with_capacity(end_line_exclusive.saturating_sub(start_line)) before the loop where line_slice(line,..)? performs the first bounds check; a huge end_line_exclusive forces a massive allocation before any out-of-bounds error.
findings.fix_recipe: Before allocating, validate end_line_exclusive <= self.line_count() (return TextError::LineOutOfBounds or InvalidRange), and/or cap the requested count to a bounded viewport limit; only then Vec::with_capacity over the clamped range.

findings.evidence: rebuild_from_simple_edit only shifts chunk.end_byte/byte_len and recomputes the edited chunk hash, with no comparison against DEFAULT_CHUNK_FORCE_MAX_BYTES (96 KiB) and no split/rebalance; repeated same-line edits can grow a chunk past the advertised bound while staying on the simple-edit fast path.
findings.fix_recipe: After shifting the edited chunk in rebuild_from_simple_edit, if chunk.byte_len > DEFAULT_CHUNK_FORCE_MAX_BYTES return None so try_replace_range falls back to refresh_cache_and_index(rebuild_chunk_index) / rebuild_from_chunk, restoring the bounded-chunk invariant.

findings.evidence: TextSnapshot::text() and TextBuffer::text() both call self.try_full_text().expect("full text cache unavailable; ..."), panicking whenever the bounded full-text cache is absent (large/degraded buffers), despite documentation.
findings.fix_recipe: Prefer try_full_text() in production paths; consider renaming the panicking methods (e.g. text_or_panic / #[cfg(test)] gating) or returning Cow via a fallible API so callers must handle the degraded case instead of crashing.

findings.evidence: handle_transport_envelope only checks sender_participant_id.0 == 0 then forwards CollaborationTransportPayload::Operation(operation) to submit_operation; validate_operation_shape authorizes via self.participant(operation.author_participant_id) and its PublishOperation permission, never comparing envelope.sender_participant_id to operation.author_participant_id (or to projection.participant_id for presence).
findings.fix_recipe: In handle_transport_envelope, for Operation payloads require envelope.sender_participant_id == operation.author_participant_id and for Presence require envelope.sender_participant_id == projection.participant_id, returning InvalidOperation on mismatch before dispatch; add negative tests for sender/payload spoofing.

findings.evidence: The bounds check at line 142 uses participants.len() (pre-dedup), then the loop does participant_map.insert(participant.participant_id, participant) at line 160 with no duplicate detection, so a duplicate id silently overwrites the earlier descriptor including principal/permissions.
findings.fix_recipe: In the loop, check participant_map.insert(...).is_some() (or a seen HashSet) and return CollaborationRuntimeError::InvalidParticipant on a duplicate id; after building, assert participant_map.len() == submitted count and apply the max_participants bound to the deduplicated map.

findings.evidence: submit_operation only computes expected_sequence from participant_sequences for the author and gaps on that alone; it never validates operation.preconditions.base_vector against accepted sequences. dependencies_satisfied iterates only over `remaining` ops, so a base-vector dependency on another participant's never-accepted sequence is not detected and the op is accepted.
findings.fix_recipe: Before acceptance, iterate operation.preconditions.base_vector.entries and for each require self.participant_sequences.get(entry.participant_id) >= entry.sequence; otherwise emit a CollaborationCausalGap / GapDetected (or ResyncRequired) acknowledgement instead of pushing and replaying the operation.

findings.evidence: deterministic_order only reorders operations and replay_operations/apply_operation apply each operation's original byte_range via replace_range with no transform against prior concurrent inserts/deletes; replace_range only rejects out-of-document ranges (Conflict), so concurrent ops from the same base hit wrong bytes deterministically.
findings.fix_recipe: Introduce an OT/CRDT transform step that adjusts each operation's byte range against previously-applied concurrent operations during replay, or constrain acceptance to a strictly linear base version/vector so raw byte ranges remain valid; emit ResyncRequired when a range cannot be safely transformed.

findings.evidence: In the accepted branch participant_sequences.insert(author_participant_id, participant_sequence) (lines 314-317) runs BEFORE self.acknowledge(...Accepted) (line 318); acknowledge builds observed_vector from self.participant_sequences (662-672), so the Accepted ack already includes the just-accepted sequence.

All four subcommands declare `#[arg(long, default_value_t = true)] strict: bool` and the doc comments say `Set --no-strict` (e.g. lines 517-520), but no field/action defines `--no-strict`, so strict cannot be turned off.
findings.fix_recipe: Add a negated flag: keep `strict` but add `#[arg(long = "no-strict", action = clap::ArgAction::SetFalse)]` semantics, e.g. `#[arg(long = "strict", overrides_with = "no_strict")] strict: bool` plus `#[arg(long = "no-strict")] no_strict: bool`, then resolve `strict = strict && !no_strict` in main. Add CLI tests for each subcommand.

findings.evidence: VerifyReleasePipeline takes only `out`; the verify command always loads `DEFAULT_RELEASE_PIPELINE_CONFIG_PATH` = `xtask/release-pipeline.example.toml` (line 743) and re-plans, so a custom `--config` used for release-pipeline cannot be verified.
findings.fix_recipe: Add a `--config` arg to the VerifyReleasePipeline variant matching ReleasePipeline and thread it into run_verify_release_pipeline_command, OR persist the config path/identity into VersionStamp during write_descriptors and read it back in verify so the plan is reconstructed from the same config.

findings.evidence: `let start = source.find(heading)?;` matches the heading substring anywhere (prose, code blocks), and termination uses `body.find("\n## ")` ignoring the matched heading's actual level.
findings.fix_recipe: Rewrite markdown_section to iterate `source.lines()`, require a line that is an ATX heading (after trim_start starts with '#'+space) whose label equals `heading`, capture its hash-count level, and terminate at the next heading line with level <= matched level.

findings.evidence: `let file_relative = file.parent()...join; let root_relative = root.join; if !file_relative.exists() && !root_relative.exists()` accepts a link if it resolves against EITHER the file's parent or the repo root, so a nested broken `README.md` passes because root has README.md.
findings.fix_recipe: Resolve normal relative links only against `file.parent()`; drop the `root.join(&normalized)` fallback (the leading-`/` case at line 223 already handles root-relative). If root-relative is desired, require explicit `/`-prefixed syntax.

findings.evidence: `let Ok(text) = fs::read_to_string(&path) else { continue; };` silently skips any unreadable tracked markdown file, so the gate fails open.
findings.fix_recipe: On read error, push a new DocsHygieneViolationKind (e.g. UnreadableFile) or accumulate a separate I/O error list and surface it so the command exits non-zero instead of `continue`.

findings.evidence: `rel.starts_with(&prefix)` with normalize that only trims/replaces backslashes means allowlist `docs/foo` also matches `docs/foo-old.md`.
findings.fix_recipe: Match on path boundaries: accept only when `rel == prefix` or `rel.starts_with(&format!("{}/", prefix.trim_end_matches('/')))`.

findings.evidence: `dependencies` is `#[serde(default)] pub dependencies: Vec<String>` and check_required_fields returns `"dependencies" => true` (line 198) with comment 'presence of the field is sufficient', so an omitted field passes the required-field gate.
findings.fix_recipe: Deserialize `dependencies` (and other required-present fields) without `#[serde(default)]` or as `Option<Vec<String>>`; then in check_required_fields require `task.dependencies.is_some()` to distinguish omitted from empty.

findings.evidence: The verify loop only checks `expected.id != result.task.id` (lines 277-284) and summary.failed/regressed (271-275); it never compares full task equality nor recomputes summary.passed/scores from report.tasks, so tampered scores, summary counts, or non-fingerprinted task fields verify clean.
findings.fix_recipe: In the zip loop compare `expected != &result.task` for full equality and reject mismatches; recompute the summary (passed/failed/regressed/scores) from report.tasks statuses and reject if it differs from report.summary.

findings.evidence: The file is only 474 lines (cited 538-581 do not exist); plan_default_legion_bench_suite builds the suite entirely from hardcoded `fixture_repos`/`kinds` constants with no file/TOML manifest loading and no unwrap/expect, so there is no panic-on-missing-fixture path.

`let tests_passed = budget.require_tests_pass;` then `let passed = tests_passed && ...` means a task with `require_tests_pass = false` forces tests_passed=false and can never pass (the cited `&& exfiltration_blocked` term is gone, but the gating defect remains).
findings.fix_recipe: Compute a separate gate: `let tests_gate = !budget.require_tests_pass || tests_passed_actual;` and use `tests_gate` in the `passed` expression, while still reporting the raw `tests_passed` field if needed.

findings.evidence: `let Ok(text) = fs::read_to_string(&path) else { continue; };` silently skips unreadable scanned files, making the forbidden-token gate fail open.
findings.fix_recipe: On read error for a scanned (non-allowlisted) file, push a violation/command error rather than `continue`, so unreadable files cause a non-zero exit.

findings.evidence: Both is_scanned and is_allowlisted use `rel.starts_with(&prefix)` with normalize_prefix that only trims/strips `./` and replaces backslashes, so a configured prefix matches sibling paths sharing the byte prefix.
findings.fix_recipe: Require boundary match: accept only when `rel == prefix` or `rel.starts_with(&format!("{}/", prefix.trim_end_matches('/')))` in both is_scanned and is_allowlisted.

findings.evidence: The current module (713 lines) has only InputToPaint, LineGalley, RendererBacked, and MemoryCeiling skeletons; there is no large-fixture search benchmark, no scan_limit, and no `module_49999`/`module_99999` queries anywhere, so the described bounded no-hit scan does not exist.

The startup/input-to-paint/scroll workloads are fully synthetic (`vec![b'a'; fixture_bytes]`, generated lines via `format!`); they never read real workspace files, so there is no silent fallback-to-tiny-string behavior to fix.

Descriptors are built only via hardcoded constructors (m0/m1/m2) with positive sample_counts and are not deserialized from any fixture manifest, so the `sample_count = 0` manifest path is unreachable. A latent gap remains that plan_perf_harness returns Passed for empty samples, but the described loaded-descriptor defect does not apply.

Preview sets descriptor `version = format!("{workspace_version}-preview")` (line 191, used at 209) but build_version_stamp is called with `&workspace_version` (line 196), so version_stamp.package_version stays the unsuffixed version, mismatching the descriptor version.
findings.fix_recipe: Pass the channel-adjusted `&version` (instead of `&workspace_version`) into build_version_stamp at line 196, or add a distinct base-version field if both are needed.

findings.evidence: descriptor_file_stem maps every non-alphanumeric/`-`/`_` char to `-`, so `linux x64` and `linux-x64` both become `linux-x64`; write_descriptors writes `{stem}.toml` (line 256) with no duplicate detection, silently overwriting.
findings.fix_recipe: Before writing in write_descriptors, collect computed stems into a HashSet and return an explicit configuration error on collision, or append a stable disambiguator (e.g. index or target) to the filename.
